title: Dalbergia peltieri
taxonomy: Dalbergia peltieri is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Dalbergia, which comprises about 250 species of trees and shrubs. The genus Dalbergia is further classified under the subfamily Faboideae. Within the Fabaceae family, Dalbergia peltieri is classified as part of the Legume order (Leguminosae). Further classification also places it within the clade Rosidae.
uses: Dalbergia peltieri is known to have several medicinal uses, primarily in traditional African medicine. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of various infections and wounds. In addition, it is also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-cancer and antioxidant activities, making it a subject of interest in modern medicine. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Dalbergia peltieri.

intro: Dalbergia peltieri, also known as Peltiroba or Pelti Dalbergia, is a plant species within the family Fabaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dalbergia peltieri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as daucosterol, isorhapontigenin, and daucosterol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dalbergia peltieri is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
